project.name: lightrag-hku
project.description: LightRAG is a Python library implementing a graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework with knowledge graph extraction and multi-mode retrieval.

nodes.languageNotes: Supports both Bun (bun:bun scripts) and Node.js/npm interchangeably for all scripts except `bun test`, which requires Bun. The heavy use of @react-sigma packages reflects the graph-visualization core of the WebUI.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json

nodes.languageNotes: Uses 'bundler' moduleResolution (Vite-compatible) instead of 'node16', and noEmit:true since Vite handles the actual transpilation.

nodes.name: file_ops.sh
nodes.summary: Core file operations library for the LightRAG setup wizard, providing functions to generate and update `.env` files, produce and patch Docker Compose files (service injection, volume management, environment overrides), and stage SSL/Redis config assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Bash associative arrays, named references (`local -n`), and process substitution extensively; functions coordinate via global state maps like `ENV_VALUES`, `COMPOSE_ENV_OVERRIDES`, and `DOCKER_SERVICE_SET`.

nodes.name: presets.sh
nodes.summary: Minimal helper library exposing `apply_preset_overwrite` to bulk-inject preset key=value pairs into the shared `ENV_VALUES` map during the setup wizard.

nodes.name: prompts.sh
nodes.summary: Interactive prompt library for the setup wizard, offering validated input loops, secret masking, clearable defaults, yes/no confirmations, and numbered choice menus with ANSI colour support.

nodes.name: storage_requirements.sh
nodes.summary: Declarative data file enumerating all supported KV, graph, vector, and doc-status storage backends alongside their required environment variables and database type labels used by the setup wizard's validation and service-generation logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Populated exclusively with Bash `declare -ag` and `declare -Ag` global arrays; no functions defined.

nodes.name: validation.sh
nodes.summary: Validation helper library for the interactive setup wizard, providing URI format checks, API key validation, port/integer validation, storage compatibility checks, OpenSearch/MongoDB config validation, auth accounts validation, and security configuration guards.

nodes.name: generate_env_file
nodes.summary: Generates or updates the `.env` file from `env.example`, activating keys present in `ENV_VALUES` while preserving commented examples, duplicate template lines, and user-added variables from previous runs.

nodes.name: generate_docker_compose
nodes.summary: Orchestrates full Docker Compose file generation: strips stale wizard-managed services, injects new service blocks from per-backend templates, applies environment/image/port/mount/depends_on overrides for the `lightrag` service, and rebuilds the top-level volumes section.

nodes.name: append_preserved_non_template_env_lines
nodes.summary: Merges user-customised or non-template environment variables from a previous `.env` file into a regenerated output file, maintaining a dedicated preserved-variables section while suppressing template-controlled keys.

nodes.name: inject_lightrag_depends_on
nodes.summary: Injects or updates the `depends_on` block of the `lightrag` service in a Compose file, adding wizard-managed backend services while preserving any user-defined non-wizard dependencies.

nodes.name: inject_service_environment_overrides
nodes.summary: Inserts or appends key=value environment entries into a named service's `environment:` block in a Compose file, creating the block if absent and matching the existing list or mapping style.

nodes.name: normalize_lightrag_restart_policy
nodes.summary: Normalises the `lightrag` service's restart policy in a Compose file, converting a top-level `restart:` key to a `deploy.restart_policy` block or vice-versa to match the target deployment mode.

nodes.name: prepare_lightrag_service_for_generated_compose
nodes.summary: Strips `env_file:` entries and `container_name:` from the `lightrag` service block before compose regeneration, ensuring the container reads its own mounted `.env` rather than having Docker Compose expand variables.

nodes.name: _strip_wizard_managed_services_and_top_level_volumes
nodes.summary: Removes wizard-managed service blocks and the entire top-level `volumes:` section from a Compose file while preserving user-defined and unchanged services, placing a regeneration marker for later re-injection.

nodes.name: _collect_referenced_named_volumes
nodes.summary: Parses a Compose file to collect all referenced named volumes (short and long-form mounts) from every service, deduplicated, for use when rebuilding the top-level volumes section.

nodes.name: apply_preset_overwrite
nodes.summary: Iterates over `key=value` argument strings and writes each pair into the global `ENV_VALUES` associative array, allowing preset profiles to bulk-override configuration defaults.

nodes.name: prompt_choice
nodes.summary: Presents a numbered menu of options with ANSI colour highlighting and an optional default, looping until the user enters a valid numeric selection.

nodes.name: prompt_until_valid
nodes.summary: Loops prompting the user with a default value until the supplied validator function accepts the input, then returns the valid value.

nodes.name: confirm_default_no
nodes.summary: Prompts for a single-key yes/no confirmation defaulting to No, looping until y/Y/n/N or Enter is pressed.

nodes.name: confirm_default_yes
nodes.summary: Prompts for a single-key yes/no confirmation defaulting to Yes, looping until y/Y/n/N or Enter is pressed.

nodes.name: validate_security_config
nodes.summary: Validates the full security configuration when AUTH_ACCOUNTS is set: checks format, rejects weak passwords, enforces TOKEN_SECRET presence, and rejects the default built-in JWT secret.

nodes.name: check_storage_compatibility
nodes.summary: Checks combinations of KV, vector, graph, and doc-status storage backends and emits yellow warnings for known incompatible or non-production-suitable combos (e.g., NetworkX for small datasets, Faiss for local-only, JSON KV for dev-only).

nodes.name: validate_opensearch_config
nodes.summary: Orchestrates full OpenSearch configuration validation: hosts format, user/password presence, password strength, and shard/replica counts as positive/non-negative integers.

nodes.name: validate_required_variables
nodes.summary: Validates that all required environment variables for the selected storage backends are present in ENV_VALUES; reports unknown storage class names and lists missing variables with a fix hint.

nodes.name: validate_mongo_vector_storage_config
nodes.summary: Validates MongoDB URI for MongoVectorDBStorage, enforcing Atlas-capable URIs (mongodb+srv:// or directConnection=true) and special handling for Docker-managed Atlas Local deployments.

nodes.name: 01-basic.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Cline AI assistant rules file capturing LightRAG's architecture, critical implementation patterns (embedding format, async, locking, event loop), development workflow, testing guidelines, and tech stack intelligence for development continuity.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git attributes file that marks all files under `lightrag/api/webui/` as binary and linguist-generated, preventing diff noise and language statistics skew from bundled frontend assets.

nodes.name: MANIFEST.in
nodes.summary: Python package manifest specifying additional non-Python files to include in the source distribution, covering requirements files, WebUI assets, static API files, and prompt sample configurations.

nodes.name: lightrag.service.example
nodes.summary: Example systemd service unit for running LightRAG as a managed background service, with memory ceilings (8 GB high / 12 GB max), virtual-environment path injection, and automatic restart on failure.

nodes.name: .env.aoi.example
nodes.summary: Example .env template for the Azure OpenAI integration, supplying the required API version, deployment names for the LLM and embedding models, API key, and endpoint URL needed to configure the azure binding.

nodes.name: requirements-offline-llm.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned pip requirements for all LLM provider dependencies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama, AWS Bedrock via aioboto3, ZhipuAI, VoyageAI, LlamaIndex) enabling offline installation workflows.

nodes.name: requirements-offline-storage.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned pip requirements for all storage backend dependencies (PostgreSQL via asyncpg/pgvector, Neo4j, OpenSearch, Milvus, MongoDB, Qdrant, Redis) enabling offline installation workflows.

nodes.name: requirements-offline.txt
nodes.summary: Complete offline pip requirements combining all LLM provider and storage backend dependencies plus document processing libs (pypdf, python-docx, openpyxl, pycryptodome) for air-gapped deployments.

nodes.name: test.sh
nodes.summary: Smart test runner shell script that probes multiple Python environments (PYTHON env var, active virtualenv, uv, .venv, system Python) in priority order and executes pytest via exec, reporting failure if no suitable environment with pytest is found.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Comprehensive interactive setup wizard for LightRAG Docker Compose and host deployments, handling LLM and embedding provider selection, storage backend configuration, SSL, service composition, VLLM integration, and environment file generation across multiple storage providers.
nodes.languageNotes: Requires Bash 4+ for associative arrays; sources modular library files from lib/ subdirectory (storage_requirements.sh, validation.sh, prompts.sh, file_ops.sh, presets.sh) for separation of concerns.

nodes.name: run_python
nodes.summary: Resolves a Python binary candidate (by path or by name via command -v), verifies it has pytest importable, and execs pytest with the forwarded arguments.

nodes.name: run_uv
nodes.summary: Checks for a uv-managed environment with a uv.lock file, verifies pytest is importable via `uv run python`, and execs pytest under the uv-managed environment.

nodes.name: validate_uri
nodes.summary: Validates a connection URI for a given database type using regex patterns; supports postgresql, neo4j (with bolt/+s/+ssc schemes), mongodb, redis, milvus/qdrant (HTTP/HTTPS), and memgraph.

nodes.name: validate_api_key
nodes.summary: Validates an API key for a given provider; openai and openrouter keys are accepted as-is, all others require at least 8 characters.

nodes.name: validate_port
nodes.summary: Validates that a port string is a numeric integer in the range 1–65535.

nodes.name: format_error
nodes.summary: Outputs a colored error message to stderr with an optional hint line using COLOR_RED/COLOR_YELLOW ANSI codes.

nodes.name: is_sensitive_env_key
nodes.summary: Returns true if an environment variable key name matches sensitive patterns: AUTH_ACCOUNTS, or any key containing API_KEY, ACCESS_KEY, PUBLIC_KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD, or TOKEN.

nodes.name: validate_sensitive_env_literals
nodes.summary: Iterates all ENV_VALUES and ensures sensitive keys do not contain `${...}` interpolation syntax, preventing accidental shell expansion of secrets in the generated .env file.

nodes.name: validate_opensearch_hosts_format
nodes.summary: Validates the OPENSEARCH_HOSTS value as comma-separated bare host:port entries (not URLs), rejecting any entry with a URL scheme or empty entries.

nodes.name: validate_opensearch_password_strength
nodes.summary: Enforces a strong OPENSEARCH_PASSWORD: minimum 8 characters with at least one uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

nodes.name: validate_auth_accounts_format
nodes.summary: Validates the AUTH_ACCOUNTS string as comma-separated user:password pairs, rejecting leading/trailing commas, empty entries, entries without a colon, or blank username/password.

nodes.name: validate_auth_accounts_password_safety
nodes.summary: Rejects weak AUTH_ACCOUNTS passwords that start with 'admin' or 'pass' (case-insensitive) to prevent common credential usage.

nodes.name: validate_auth_accounts_runtime_config
nodes.summary: Validates AUTH_ACCOUNTS format at runtime, delegating to validate_auth_accounts_format and emitting a user-friendly error with bcrypt hash hint.

nodes.name: whitelist_exposes_api_routes
nodes.summary: Checks whether any entry in the WHITELIST_PATHS value exposes `/api` or any `/api/*` subtree, used to warn about open API route access.

nodes.name: check_docker_availability
nodes.summary: Checks that Docker and the Docker Compose plugin are available in PATH; emits errors if either is missing.

nodes.name: init_colors
nodes.summary: Initializes ANSI color variables (RESET, BOLD, BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW, RED) when stdout is a terminal and NO_COLOR is unset.

nodes.name: reset_state
nodes.summary: Resets all associative arrays and scalar state variables to empty/default values, enabling a clean re-run of the setup wizard within the same shell session.

nodes.name: validate_runtime_target
nodes.summary: Validates the LIGHTRAG_RUNTIME_TARGET value, accepting only 'host' or 'compose' and emitting a descriptive error for any other value.

nodes.name: normalize_provider_binding_hosts
nodes.summary: Backfills sentinel host values for bedrock and gemini LLM/embedding bindings when LLM_BINDING_HOST or EMBEDDING_BINDING_HOST are missing, preventing openai URL leakage into regenerated .env files.

nodes.name: load_existing_env_if_present
nodes.summary: Loads existing .env file defaults into ENV_VALUES if the file exists, then applies deprecation cleanup and normalization before snapshotting the original values.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS v3 configuration for the LightRAG WebUI, extending the theme with CSS variable-based color tokens, border radius primitives, accordion keyframe animations, and fully customised @tailwindcss/typography prose styles for dark-mode support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ES module `export default` syntax; CSS custom properties (var(--*)) decouple design tokens from Tailwind so the same config works for both light and dark themes.

nodes.name: hash_password
nodes.summary: Hashes a plaintext password with bcrypt and returns an AUTH_ACCOUNTS-ready string prefixed with `{bcrypt}`.

nodes.name: verify_password
nodes.summary: Verifies a plaintext password against a stored credential, handling both bcrypt-prefixed hashes (using bcrypt.checkpw) and legacy plain-text equality checks.

nodes.name: Chart.yaml
nodes.summary: Helm chart descriptor for the LightRAG Kubernetes deployment, declaring the chart name, type (application), semantic version (0.1.1), appVersion (1.0.0), and maintainer contact information.

nodes.name: values.yaml
nodes.summary: Default Helm values for the LightRAG chart, configuring replica count, container image, Recreate update strategy, ClusterIP service on port 9621, CPU/memory resource limits, persistent storage volumes (10 Gi RAG storage + 5 Gi inputs), and environment variables for LLM/embedding bindings and PostgreSQL/Neo4j/Qdrant database connections.

nodes.name: README_CLEAN_LLM_QUERY_CACHE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive user guide for the LLM Query Cache Cleanup Tool, covering supported storage backends (JSON, Redis, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, OpenSearch), cache key format, prerequisite setup, usage commands, and interactive workflow for cleaning query-mode caches.

nodes.name: README_MIGRATE_LLM_CACHE.md
nodes.summary: User guide for the LLM Cache Migration Tool, documenting procedures to migrate LightRAG extraction caches (entity extraction and summary caches) between different KV storage implementations with step-by-step interactive workflow.

nodes.name: entity_type_prompt.sample.yml
nodes.summary: Sample YAML configuration providing entity type guidance and multi-shot extraction examples for LightRAG's NLP extraction pipeline, supporting both plain-text and JSON extraction modes with annotated fictional story examples.

nodes.name: UserCustomizePrompts.md
nodes.summary: Minimal documentation stub indicating that the prompts folder is designated for storing user-defined custom prompt template files.

nodes.name: batch_eval.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI Batch API evaluation script that compares two sets of RAG query answers using GPT-4o-mini, scoring results on Comprehensiveness, Diversity, and Empowerment criteria for LightRAG research benchmarking.

nodes.name: batch_eval
nodes.summary: Constructs and submits OpenAI Batch API requests comparing two answer sets against a query file, evaluating Comprehensiveness, Diversity, and Empowerment criteria per question.

nodes.name: Step_0.py
nodes.summary: Dataset preprocessing script for LightRAG's reproduction pipeline that reads JSONL files, extracts unique context entries preserving order, and saves them as JSON files for downstream question generation.

nodes.name: extract_unique_contexts
nodes.summary: Scans a directory of JSONL files, deduplicates context field values while preserving insertion order, and writes unique contexts to per-file JSON output files.

nodes.name: Step_2.py
nodes.summary: Question generation script using GPT-4o and GPT2 tokenizer to produce structured research questions from dataset context summaries, driving LightRAG benchmarking dataset creation.

nodes.name: openai_complete_if_cache
nodes.summary: Wrapper that constructs an OpenAI chat completion request from prompt, optional system prompt, and message history, returning the response content string.

nodes.name: get_summary
nodes.summary: Extracts a token-limited summary from a long context string by selecting tokens from the middle and end of the tokenized sequence using GPT2 tokenizer.

nodes.name: regen_native_docx_golden.py
nodes.summary: Maintenance script that regenerates byte-equivalence golden test fixtures for the native DOCX sidecar pipeline by running mocked parse scenarios against LightRAG's native parser and capturing output artifacts for deterministic test comparison.

nodes.name: _regen
nodes.summary: Async function that iterates DOCX test scenarios, stubs docx block extraction and datetime via mocks, runs LightRAG's native parse pipeline, and copies produced artifacts to golden fixture directories.

nodes.name: set_version.py
nodes.summary: Release automation utility that updates __version__ and __api_version__ constants in lightrag/_version.py from CLI arguments, supporting automatic v-prefix stripping for core version normalization.

nodes.name: update_assignment
nodes.summary: Replaces a Python string assignment value (e.g., __version__ = "x") in file content using multiline regex substitution, raising ValueError on failure to match exactly once.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point parsing --core-version and optional --api-version arguments, normalizing version strings, and writing updated constants to the version file.

nodes.name: redis.conf.template
nodes.summary: Redis server configuration template for LightRAG's bundled Redis service, configuring network binding, RDB and AOF persistence, 4GB memory limit with noeviction policy, and 10K client connection limit.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS stylesheet for the LightRAG WebUI defining Tailwind v4 theme variables with CSS custom properties for light and dark modes, including semantic color tokens, sidebar palette, chart colors, custom scrollbar styling, and KaTeX math formula rendering overrides.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Tailwind v4 @import and @plugin directives alongside @theme inline blocks and @custom-variant for dark mode; all design tokens are CSS custom properties bridged into Tailwind's color system.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand why LightRAG exists and what makes it different from naive vector-search RAG. It introduces the core concept: extract entities and relationships from documents, build a knowledge graph, then support five retrieval modes (local, global, hybrid, naive, mix) to augment LLM responses with structured domain knowledge. This gives you the mental map you need before diving into code.

tour.title: The LightRAG Orchestrator
tour.description: lightrag.py is the heart of the library: the LightRAG class composes storage backends, LLM/embedding functions, and all insert/query/delete operations through a mixin architecture. Its public API (ainsert, aquery, adelete, ainsert_file) is what library users call directly. Understanding this class reveals the top-level contract and shows how all subsystems connect. addon_params.py provides the ObservableAddonParams dict that fires callbacks when runtime parameters change.

tour.title: Core Data Models
tour.description: base.py defines the abstract storage interfaces — BaseVectorStorage, BaseKVStorage, BaseGraphStorage, BaseDocStatusStorage — that every backend must implement, alongside shared data models like QueryParam and DocProcessingStatus. types.py complements this with domain types used across the codebase. These two files are the vocabulary the entire system speaks; understanding them unlocks every other module.
tour.languageLesson: Python abstract base classes (ABCs) enforce interface contracts via @abstractmethod decorators. Subclasses that omit any abstract method raise TypeError at instantiation time, catching missing implementations before they cause subtle runtime failures.

tour.title: Knowledge Graph Operations
tour.description: operate.py is the algorithmic core: it orchestrates entity and relationship extraction from text chunks, merges nodes and edges into the knowledge graph, and implements all five query modes. prompt.py acts as a companion, providing the central PROMPTS registry with every LLM prompt template used during extraction and query synthesis. This pair is what truly differentiates LightRAG from ordinary vector-search RAG.
tour.languageLesson: LightRAG uses Python asyncio throughout. asyncio.gather() batches many LLM calls concurrently on a single thread — entity extraction for hundreds of chunks runs in parallel, dramatically reducing wall-clock ingestion time compared to sequential HTTP calls.

tour.title: Document Ingestion Pipeline
tour.description: pipeline.py is a mixin that isolates the parse-enqueue-extract logic from the main LightRAG class, keeping each file focused on one responsibility. It receives raw documents, routes them through format parsers, produces text chunks via the chunker package, and queues them for knowledge-graph extraction. utils_pipeline.py provides document identity hashing, path resolution, sidecar URIs, and chunk normalization helpers that the pipeline depends on.

tour.title: Utility Foundations
tour.description: utils.py is the most-imported file in the project (fan-in: 42) for good reason — it supplies logging, the async work-queue manager, the EmbeddingFunc wrapper with dimension caching, LLM response caching, tokenization, text splitting, and result formatting. Nearly every other module depends on it, making it the true bedrock layer. constants.py and namespace.py round out the shared primitives that prevent magic strings and circular imports.

tour.title: Pluggable Storage Architecture
tour.description: LightRAG supports over a dozen storage backends, selected at runtime through a factory pattern in kg/factory.py: a plain dict maps backend name strings ("networkx", "neo4j", "postgres", "faiss", "redis"...) to implementation classes. kg/__init__.py re-exports all implementations and exposes a verify_storage_implementation helper that confirms a class satisfies the abstract interface from base.py before it is registered or instantiated.
tour.languageLesson: The factory pattern in Python is commonly a plain dict mapping string keys to classes, then calling cls(**kwargs) to instantiate the selected backend. This eliminates long if-elif chains and makes adding a new backend a one-line dict entry — no modification to the dispatch logic needed.

tour.title: Storage Backend Implementations
tour.description: Three concrete backends illustrate the range of deployment options. networkx_impl.py is the zero-dependency default: in-process graph storage serialised to GraphML, ideal for development. postgres_impl.py is the full production stack: asyncpg for async I/O, pgvector for embedding similarity, and Apache AGE for Cypher-based graph queries — all in one Postgres instance. faiss_impl.py represents the pure-vector path with cosine similarity over L2-normalised IndexFlatIP embeddings and atomic file persistence.
tour.languageLesson: asyncpg uses Python asyncio natively (no thread pool). Every database query yields the event loop back to other coroutines while waiting for the network round-trip, letting the Postgres backend serve many concurrent requests without spawning OS threads.

tour.title: LLM Provider Bindings
tour.description: The llm/ package contains async bindings for OpenAI, Ollama, Azure, Gemini, Bedrock, Anthropic, and more — each wrapping its provider SDK into the uniform async (prompt, **kwargs) -> str interface that operate.py calls. One level above, llm_roles.py provides the _RoleLLMMixin: it lets operators assign different LLM providers to distinct pipeline stages (extraction, query synthesis, vision) and hot-swap them at runtime without restarting the server.

tour.title: REST API Server
tour.description: lightrag_server.py is the FastAPI application factory and CLI entrypoint: it wires all routers, configures CORS and middleware, and initialises the LightRAG engine from environment variables. document_routes.py handles file upload, text insert, scan, delete, and pipeline control; query_routes.py exposes all five retrieval modes with both streaming and non-streaming responses. auth.py layers optional JWT authentication on top — toggled off for development, enforced in production.

tour.title: React WebUI Frontend
tour.description: lightrag_webui/ is a React 19 SPA with hash-based routing (AppRouter.tsx) and four main views: document management, knowledge graph explorer, retrieval testing, and API reference. App.tsx bootstraps health-check polling and manages feature-tab state across the UI. lightrag.ts is the central HTTP client shared by all components — it handles authentication headers, JSON serialisation, and real-time Server-Sent Events for streamed query responses.
tour.languageLesson: Hash-based routing (React Router HashRouter) keeps the URL fragment (#/path) entirely client-side. This avoids 404 errors when hosting a SPA on a static file server or CDN that cannot perform server-side URL rewriting.

tour.title: Knowledge Graph Explorer
tour.description: KnowledgeGraphPage.tsx hosts LightRAG's most visually distinctive feature: an interactive force-directed graph of the extracted entity-relationship network. GraphViewer.tsx wraps a sigma.js canvas with pan, zoom, and node-click interactions. useGraph.ts encapsulates all data fetching, label filtering, and sigma instance lifecycle in a custom hook, cleanly separating data logic from presentation so the graph can be reused across different pages.

tour.title: Containerization and Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile uses a three-stage build: a Bun stage compiles the React WebUI, a uv/Python builder installs Python dependencies and pre-populates the tiktoken cache, and a minimal runtime stage merges both artifacts into the final image. docker-compose.yml exposes port 9621 and mounts persistent volumes for RAG data and inputs. env.example documents all 1000+ configuration settings — LLM provider, storage backend, authentication, SSL, and logging — making it the definitive reference for production deployments.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM directives. Each stage produces an intermediate layer; final COPY --from=<stage> instructions copy only the runtime artifacts forward, discarding compilers, build tools, and dev dependencies. This typically reduces production image size by 60-80%.
